title: Add Universal Launch Report tab with live canister metrics

requirements.text: Create a new authenticated Universal Launch Report page/component in the KD Platform area that renders a “KD.WebAI NOC v13.1 - Universal Launch Report” view similar to the user-provided React example, using the existing KD/NOC Tailwind styling tokens (e.g., bg-kd-bg, bg-kd-panel, text-kd-text) and English-only user-facing text.

quotes: I'll integrate this page into your existing React dashboard as a new Universal Launch Report tab, while also making it fetch live data from your deployed canisters using the ICP JavaScript agent.

acceptanceCriteria: A new report UI is available as a React component/page and visually matches the existing KD/NOC styling (kd-* classes) rather than raw default Tailwind grays.
All user-facing strings on the report page are in English.
The page requires Internet Identity authentication (same gating behavior as KDDashboardPage/NOCPage).

requirements.text: Add a new route for the Universal Launch Report under the existing TanStack Router configuration (frontend/src/App.tsx) and add an in-app navigation entry from the KD Platform dashboard so users can open the report as a new tab/page in the dashboard flow.

quotes: Add the Tab to Your Router
Add a navigation link in your sidebar or header.

acceptanceCriteria: A new TanStack Router route exists for the report page (do not use react-router-dom <Route>).
KD Platform Dashboard (frontend/src/pages/KDDashboardPage.tsx) includes a navigation control that routes to the report page.
Navigation works in both desktop and mobile layouts without breaking existing routes (/kd-platform/dashboard, /kd-platform/noc).

requirements.text: Fetch and display live data on the Universal Launch Report page using the existing React Query hooks/actor flow (frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts + useActor), including: treasury status/TVL (from getTreasuryStatus), Omni-Mesh status (from getOmniMeshStatus), cycles balance (new query), and DAO proposals (from getDaoProposals). Configure polling/refresh every 10 seconds for these report metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: The report page uses React Query (not manual setInterval state management) to load the required metrics via the backend actor.
The report metrics automatically refresh at least every 10 seconds while the page is open.
Loading and error states are handled (e.g., a loading indicator and a non-crashing error message/log) and the page remains usable if one metric fails.
DAO proposals render as a list using the live proposal fields returned by getDaoProposals (id/title/description/votes).

requirements.text: Expose a backend query method to return the canister cycles balance, and wire it into the frontend report. Add a new public query function on the existing single Motoko actor (backend/main.mo) that returns cycles balance, gated with the same RBAC pattern used by other NOC query methods.

quotes: Ensure Your Canister Interfaces Expose the Required Methods
public query func getCyclesBalance() : async Nat { Cycles.balance() }

acceptanceCriteria: backend/main.mo defines a new query method getCyclesBalance that returns the canister’s current cycles balance and enforces user-level access (same AccessControl.hasPermission(..., #user) pattern as getTreasuryStatus/getOmniMeshStatus).
Frontend compiles and can call getCyclesBalance via the typed actor bindings without TypeScript errors.
No state migration (backend/migration.mo) is introduced/required for this change.

requirements.text: Update the frontend’s canister/type bindings so the new backend method (getCyclesBalance) is available to TypeScript imports already used in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts (i.e., ensure the generated/declared backend interface includes the new method and build succeeds).

quotes: Update canister.js (or .ts) to Export All Canister Actors
adjust imports to your canister interfaces

acceptanceCriteria: TypeScript build succeeds with no missing-method errors for getCyclesBalance.
The report page can call the backend actor method for cycles balance using the same actor creation mechanism already used by existing hooks (do not modify immutable hook files).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor (all logic in backend/main.mo).

Do not edit files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui; compose them instead.
Use TanStack Router patterns already present in frontend/src/App.tsx (do not introduce react-router-dom routes).
nonGoals: Do not add/require Vercel deployment changes or CI/CD steps.
Do not implement WebSockets or real-time push; polling via React Query is sufficient.
Do not add third-party authentication providers beyond Internet Identity.
Do not introduce additional backend canisters/services; implement required new method(s) on the existing backend actor.
